title: Polypodium × rothmaleri
taxonomy: Polypodium × rothmaleri is a hybrid plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern and is known to be a sterile hybrid, meaning it does not produce spores. The classification of Polypodium × rothmaleri includes it being a member of the genus Polypodium, which comprises a group of terrestrial ferns. Polypodium × rothmaleri is often considered a cultivar or a hybrid of two Polypodium species. The exact classification of this hybrid may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a member of the Polypodiaceae family.
uses: Polypodium × rothmaleri is a hybrid fern species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region. Its medicinal uses include being used as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent to treat various skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating infections. In addition, Polypodium × rothmaleri has been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for fever. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications and benefits.

preparations: Polypodium × rothmaleri is not commonly referenced in the context of herbal medicine, but it may be used in some traditional or folk remedies, often in combination with other plants. intro: Polypodium × rothmaleri, also known as the Rothmaler's polypody or Rothmaler's polypodium, is a sterile hybrid of the fern species Polypodium australiense and Polypodium decumanum, characterized by its unique, terrestrial growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium × rothmaleri include various polyphenolic compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, as well as polysaccharides and other secondary metabolites. In traditional herbal medicine, Polypodium × rothmaleri is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin disorders.
